subject: current-affairs
description: Other International Awards & Prizes MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Which award is named after a US publisher and given for Journalism, Literature and Music?
mcqs.explanation: The Pulitzer Prize is named after US publisher Joseph Pulitzer and conferred annually in the USA for Journalism, Literature and Music.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Pulitzer Prize first instituted?
mcqs.explanation: The Pulitzer Prize was instituted in 1917.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Pulitzer Prize awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Pulitzer Prize is awarded annually in the United States.

mcqs.q: Which award is often called 'Asia's Nobel Prize'?
mcqs.explanation: The Magsaysay Award is widely known as 'Asia's Nobel Prize'.

mcqs.q: The Magsaysay Award is named after a former President of which country?
mcqs.explanation: The Magsaysay Award is named after Ramon Magsaysay, former President of the Philippines.

mcqs.q: On which date is the Magsaysay Award conferred annually?
mcqs.explanation: The Magsaysay Award is conferred annually on 31 August.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Magsaysay Award instituted?
mcqs.explanation: The Ramon Magsaysay Award was instituted in 1957.

mcqs.q: Which prize is awarded for 'Progress in Religion'?
mcqs.explanation: The Templeton Prize is awarded for progress in religion.

mcqs.q: After whom is the Templeton Prize named?
mcqs.explanation: The Templeton Prize is named after John M. Templeton, a Presbyterian of Nassau, Bahamas.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Templeton Prize instituted?
mcqs.explanation: The Templeton Prize was instituted in 1972.

mcqs.q: The Man Booker Prize is conferred annually for excellence in:
mcqs.explanation: The Man Booker Prize is the UK's most prestigious literary award for fiction.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Man Booker Prize first awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Man Booker Prize was first awarded in 1968.

mcqs.q: In which country is the Man Booker Prize awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Man Booker Prize is awarded in the United Kingdom.

mcqs.q: The Man Booker International Prize was first awarded in:
mcqs.explanation: The Man Booker International Prize was first awarded in 2005 (instituted in 2004).

mcqs.q: How frequently was the Man Booker International Prize originally awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Man Booker International Prize was originally awarded every two years to a living author of any nationality.

mcqs.q: The Oscar Award is also known as the:
mcqs.explanation: The Oscar Award is officially known as the Academy Award.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first Oscar Award given?
mcqs.explanation: The first Oscar / Academy Awards ceremony was held in 1929.

mcqs.q: Which body confers the Oscar Award?
options: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
mcqs.explanation: The Oscar is conferred by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences.

mcqs.q: The Emmy Awards are given for excellence in:
mcqs.explanation: The Emmy Awards recognise excellence in television (Primetime, Daytime, Creative Arts and Engineering).

mcqs.q: The Grammy Awards recognise achievements in:
mcqs.explanation: The Grammy Awards are the most prestigious awards in music and the recording arts.

mcqs.q: Which body organises the Grammy Awards?
mcqs.explanation: The Grammy Awards are organised by The Recording Academy.

mcqs.q: The UN Human Rights Award was instituted in:
mcqs.explanation: The UN Human Rights Award was instituted in 1966.

mcqs.q: How frequently is the UN Human Rights Award given?
mcqs.explanation: The UN Human Rights Award is given every 5 years for individual contributions to the establishment of human rights.

mcqs.q: Which international award is popularly called the 'Little Nobel'?
mcqs.explanation: The UNESCO Peace Award is widely called the 'Little Nobel' Prize.

mcqs.q: The Pritzker Architecture Prize is regarded as the highest accolade in:
mcqs.explanation: The Pritzker Architecture Prize is considered the highest international award in architecture.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Pritzker Architecture Prize first awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Pritzker Architecture Prize was first awarded in 1979 (instituted 1978).

mcqs.q: Which foundation sponsors the Pritzker Architecture Prize?
mcqs.explanation: The Pritzker Architecture Prize is sponsored by the Hyatt Foundation of Los Angeles.

mcqs.q: In which year was the King Faisal Foundation Prize established?
mcqs.explanation: The King Faisal Foundation was established in 1976 by the eight sons of the late King Faisal ibn Abd Al Aziz of Saudi Arabia.

mcqs.q: The King Faisal Prizes for Service to Islam, Islamic Studies and Arabic Literature were first awarded in:
mcqs.explanation: The first King Faisal Prizes were awarded in 1979.

mcqs.q: In which year was Medicine added as a category to the King Faisal Foundation Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Medicine was added to the King Faisal Prize in 1981.

mcqs.q: In which year was Science added as a category to the King Faisal Foundation Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Science was added to the King Faisal Prize in 1982.

mcqs.q: Which country awards the King Faisal Foundation Prize?
mcqs.explanation: The King Faisal Foundation Prize is awarded by Saudi Arabia.
